assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing trustify-backend entry in the Repository Registry unchanged (same Role, Serena Instance, Path values as in claude-md-configured.md)
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 7 contains: '| trustify-backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/trustify-backend |' which matches the reference values exactly (Role: 'Rust backend service', Serena Instance: 'serena_backend', Path: '/home/user/trustify-backend').

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md adds a new trustify-ui entry to the Repository Registry with serena_ui as the Serena Instance
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md line 8 contains: '| trustify-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/trustify-ui |' — a new row with trustify-ui repository and serena_ui as the Serena Instance.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Jira Configuration fields unchanged — Project key remains TC, Cloud ID, Feature issue type ID remain as in claude-md-configured.md
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 12-16 contain all five Jira fields matching the reference exactly: Project key: TC, Cloud ID: 2b9e35e3-6bd3-4cec-b838-f4249ee02432, Feature issue type ID: 10142, Git Pull Request custom field: customfield_10875, GitHub Issue custom field: customfield_10747.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md explicitly identifies which entries were preserved (existing) and which were added (new)
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md has a '## Preserved (existing -- unchanged)' section (lines 3-17) listing the trustify-backend row, all Jira fields, and Code Intelligence content as preserved. It has a '## Added (new)' section (lines 19-25) listing the trustify-ui row and serena_ui limitation as added. Both categories are explicitly labeled.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md notes that serena_backend was already configured and serena_ui is newly discovered
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md line 9: 'serena_backend -- Already configured in Repository Registry.' with status 'Already present in existing Project Configuration. No changes needed.' (line 11). Line 13: 'serena_ui -- Newly discovered. Not yet in Repository Registry.' with status 'Added to Repository Registry and Code Intelligence Limitations.' (line 15).

assertion_results.text: The existing Code Intelligence section content (naming convention, example, serena_backend limitations) is preserved — only new serena_ui content is added
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 20-29 preserve the naming convention ('Tools are prefixed by Serena instance name: `mcp__<instance>__<tool>`.'), the serena_backend find_symbol example (lines 25-29), and the serena_backend limitation (line 33: 'serena_backend: rust-analyzer may take 30-60 seconds to index on first use'). The only addition is line 34: 'serena_ui: No known limitations'. changes-log.md also confirms: 'Tool naming convention and example preserved verbatim' and lists serena_ui limitation under Added.

assertion_results.text: The skill offers Security Configuration opt-in since the existing CLAUDE.md has no '## Security Configuration' section, and respects the user's decline — no Security Configuration section appears in the output
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md lines 24-26 document that the user was asked about security triage configuration and declined: 'User was asked whether to enable security triage configuration. User declined. Security Configuration section was not added.' claude-md-result.md contains no '## Security Configuration' section (the file ends at line 34 with the Limitations entries). changes-log.md lines 28-30 also confirm: 'User declined security triage opt-in. Section was not included.'
